intent: processJobEmailsMain exceeds the Apps Script 6-min execution limit (time-based trigger failing repeatedly). Add wall-clock deadline guard to the per-email loop (stop launching new emails near ~290s, defer remainder to next hourly run) and cap in-process rate-limit/backoff sleeps so they cannot blow the budget. Keep MAX_EMAILS_PER_RUN=10 (run self-limits via deadline).
scope: src/features/job-finder/main.js (per-email loop deadline guard), src/core/api-service.js (cap in-process rate-limit pre-wait + backoff sleeps), src/core/config.js (deadline constant if needed), tests-local/

evidence.limit: Apps Script consumer time-based trigger hard cap = 6 min. Error: 'Exceeded maximum execution time'.
evidence.cause: Prior leg drop-precheck-bump-throughput set MAX_EMAILS_PER_RUN=10 and removed the cheap precheck -> 10 full Gemini extractions/run. In-process sleeps compound: api-service.js:196 Utilities.sleep(rateLimitStatus.waitTime+100) waits out rate-limit window inside execution; exponential backoff Utilities.sleep(backoffTime) on retries; main.js:467 _jfUtils().sleep(500) between emails. No wall-clock deadline -> run exceeds 6 min.
evidence.boundary: src/CLAUDE.md hexagonal invariant in force: main.js (feature) must use ports for sleep; api-service.js is core (Utilities.sleep allowed there). Date.now() for the deadline is allowed (not a platform SDK).
